titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Violi Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 9, 2019 at 04:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 24, 25, 29 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 30 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eleven series: I. Correspondence, circa 1970s-2011. II. Journal and Notebooks, 1965-2011. III. Publishing, 1970-1987. IV. Manuscripts, circa 1969-1999. V. Publications by Paul Violi, circa 1997-2000. VI. Education and teaching papers, undated. VII. Photographs, undated. VIII. Papers relating to Kenneth Koch, circa 1990s. IX. Other Papers, 1960s-2006. X. Audiovisual Material, undated. XI. Born Digital Material, circa 1975-2004.

bioghist.head: Paul Violi
bioghist.p: Paul Violi (1944-2011) was an American poet and small press publisher. He co-founded Swollen Magpie Press, taught at Columbia University and the New School, and won numerous awards for his writing including two poetry fellowships from the National Endowment for the Arts. His works include Overnight (2007), Fracas (1998), and Splurge (1982).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Violi Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, teaching material, newspaper clippings, audiovisual material, student work, juvenilia, and printed materials relating to the life and work of American poet Paul Violi. Materials document his writing, his relationships with other writers and artists including Charles North, Tony Towle, and Dale Devereux Barker, and his activities as co-founder of Swollen Magpie Press.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paul Violi Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence related to Violi's life and work as a poet and publisher, including letters from Tony Towle, Ira Cohen, and Ted Berrigan.

did.unittitle: American Academy of Arts and Letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Catalogs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Fagin manuscript with comments by Paul Violi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Violi assisted Fuller while he wrote the text to Tetrascroll

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Paul Violi manuscript with comments by Gallup.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily clippings of Paul Violi's Herald writings with some corporate business records.

did.unittitle: New York Foundatation for the Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on O'Hara's correspondence with John Ashbery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Paul Violi's manuscripts with Schjeldahl's comments.

did.unittitle: Presentation script (a talk presented with Paul Violi)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscript for Leap Year: Poems 1968-1978 , inscribed broadside "Translation," and assorted manuscripts, some with notes and corrections in the hand of Paul Violi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes annotated manuscript for The Year of the Olive Oil , Cadenza , and No Other Way: Selected Prose 1975-1996 .

did.unittitle: Paul Violi manuscripts with Charles North edits
Manuscripts - The History of the Invitation
Paul Violi manuscripts with Tony Towle edits
Paul Violi manuscripts with Tony Towle edits

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains journals and notebooks that feature writings, drafts, and ephemera related to Violi's poetry.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, reviews, business papers, and other documentation related to Violi's work as a publisher.

did.unittitle: Bibliographies and curricula vitae - Multiple versions
New York Times - Manuscripts and layouts, several issues
SMP (Swollen Magpie Press) - Assorted items, including a manuscript by Rebecca Wright

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, annotated revisions, proofs, notes, and writings related to Violi's poetry.

did.unittitle: [Untitled] - Poems and translations of work by Juan Ramon Jimenez

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand-drawn map of Nigeria.

did.unittitle: Q - Handwritten quotations from a variety of authors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invoices from bookstores, instructions and invoices from the printer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Paul Violi, family and friends, Ravi Shankar, and Bob Dylan. Also contains school report cards and agreement to write for Contemporary Authors .

did.unittitle: Drafts for statement for "The Poetry Project 1988 Symposium"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes business records, paste-ups for title page, and annotated manuscript.

did.unittitle: "6 White Horses" manuscript with Peter Schjeldahl's proofs
Spanish translations with notes from translator
Untitled manuscript with handwritten list of recipients

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a printed musical composition with Paul Violi poems set to music by Scott Gendel, with notes by composer.

did.unittitle: "One Extreme to Another" manuscript, original artwork, and correspondence from Chris Bloor
Collage and marker drawings by Paul Violi

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain arrangement found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains broadsides and other publications by Paul Violi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes etching by Louise Hamlin.

did.unittitle: "On an Acura Integra" signed copies from edition of fifty

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes, syllabi, and other documents related to Violi's teaching experiences.

did.unittitle: Education - Boston university - Notes, class handouts, diploma
Education - Institute for Architecture and Urban Affairs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes for Colin Rowe's "The Architecture of the Italians: 16th century."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Paul Violi and other individuals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs by Andy Grundberg and Rudy Burckhardt.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, clippings, correspondence and other papers related to Paul Violi and Kenneth Koch.

did.unittitle: Kenneth Koch's "A Possible World: Poems" manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes from Paul Violi, along with Kenneth Koch obituaries and other laid-in items.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, catalog, and poster for 1993 exhibition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes assorted brochures and handwritten daily chronicle of trip to England for Koch exhibition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes, writings, clippings, printed material, audiovisual material, born digital material, and ephemera related to Violi's life and travels.

did.unittitle: England and Wales - daily chronicle of trip
Cards and letters from Paul Violi to his family
Juvenalia and maps used while traveling in Nigeria, Europe and northwest Africa
Items related to the Selected Service and Paul Violi's 1977 work with a survey polling company
Memorial book, service announcement card, and New York Times obituary for Rudy Burckhardt
Items related to Julian Jaynes and research on consciousness
Joseph Ceravolo, Spring in this World of Poor Mutts
Raymond Roussel, How I Wrote Certain of My Books
"[Jim] Brodey's Bill" cards and other items
New York Times reviews, clippings, and other items
Catalog for "Poets and Painters" exhibition at the Denver Art Museum
Material from printed books and serials

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains audio and video recordings, including documentation of poetry readings by Violi and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes readings by Paul Violi, Allen Ginsberg, John Giorno, William Burroughs, Diane de Prima, Michael McCLure, Ed Dorn, Robert Duncan, Anne Waldman, and Taylor Meade.
